repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue PartialID entry gives a selected cascade/RD cutoff endpoint sharp set; closest local entries are pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec and ManskiNonparametricBounds, which concern unrelated smoothness/MSM or bounded-outcome policy-value bounds.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2018OptimalInference and KolesarRothe2018DiscreteRD cover fixed-target smooth/discrete RD honest endpoint logic, not a population cost-quality selected cascade cutoff. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/1511.06028 and https://arxiv.org/abs/1606.04086.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo PartialID proposal states same-frontier non-representability of a selected local net value or a critical curvature sign-certification index.

published_prior_art.cite: Kotte2026UCCI selects an isotonic calibrated escalation threshold by constrained cost minimization, and Bouchard2026EscalationWorthIt characterizes cascade cost-quality frontier geometry; neither states a same-K_P,Q_P non-representability theorem for V_*. Sources: https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.18796 and https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06350.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue entry gives finite-atom active-set geometry for curvature-indexed selected endpoint bounds.

published_prior_art.cite: The closest published analogues are optimal-recovery and honest-CI smoothness classes in ArmstrongKolesar2018OptimalInference and ArmstrongKolesar2020SimpleHonest, but I found no selected cascade/RD active-set characterization for M-indexed endpoint bounds. Source: https://arxiv.org/abs/1606.01200.

repo_prior_art.cite: No repo PartialID or Stat entry combines same-sample selected cascade cutoff inference with generated calibrated-score drift.

published_prior_art.cite: CalonicoCattaneoFarrell2018OptimalBandwidthRBC, ArmstrongKolesar2018BandwidthSnooping, DongKolesar2021RunningVariableMeasurementError, BachocPreinerstorferSteinberger2016PostSelection, and Pernot2023IsotonicStratification cover adjacent fixed-cutoff, bandwidth-selection, generated-running-variable, post-selection, or isotonic-mass issues, not this combined selected-threshold honest CI. Sources include https://arxiv.org/abs/1809.00236, https://arxiv.org/abs/1412.0267, https://arxiv.org/abs/2111.07388, https://arxiv.org/abs/1611.01043, https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.05180.

novelty_flags.label: proposal-level comparator bookkeeping
novelty_flags.one_line: The handoff says the required SC6 comparator promise table is missing while the proposal names Kotte2026UCCI, Bouchard2026EscalationWorthIt, ArmstrongKolesar2018OptimalInference, and KolesarRothe2018DiscreteRD as comparators.

novelty_flags.label: related_work / RD policy-learning comparator
novelty_flags.one_line: ZhangBenMichaelImai2022SafePolicyRD is in the bibliography and is a close RD cutoff policy-learning comparator, but it is not positioned in related_work or any statement gap.

soundness_flags.label: prop:critical-curvature
soundness_flags.one_line: The two-law same-frontier separation needs an explicit non-singleton/slack condition on [L_*(P,M),U_*(P,M)]; the current assumptions also allow singleton endpoint regimes.

soundness_flags.label: conj:active-set-geometry
soundness_flags.one_line: The claim that M_*(P) is attained at a first active interpolation constraint needs the sign-certification regime stated explicitly, e.g. 0 outside the M=0 interval and finite positive M_*.

soundness_flags.label: ass:generated-score-localization
soundness_flags.one_line: The local sup-norm estimability and sqrt(N)(h_n^2+r_n/h_n)->0 requirement for an isotonic/generated calibration score is load-bearing for the CI rung and is stronger than the cited generated-score literature justifies.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:selected-sharp-set / main identification kernel
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Sharpness is accepted only conditional on the imposed C^2 curvature-budget extension class and on holding the selected threshold fixed inside the observationally equivalent class.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The result is a field-tier selected-target sharp bound under the stated smooth-extension model, not a flagship derivation of the frontier from primitives.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:postselect-honest-ci
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The inference rung is limited to the locally dense score regime and does not claim honest post-selection inference for the genuinely atomic isotonic-score regime.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Coverage is only a conditional dense-regime conjecture with selection margin and generated-score localization assumptions.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Emit the missing SC6 comparator promise table and add a statement-level comparator map tying Kotte2026UCCI and Bouchard2026EscalationWorthIt to the exact K_P,Q_P separation claim.

Add the missing nondegeneracy/sign-certification hypotheses for prop:critical-curvature and conj:active-set-geometry, including a condition that [L_*(P,M),U_*(P,M)] is non-singleton where the two-law separation is invoked.

Either prove a primitive isotonic/local-calibration rate that implies ass:generated-score-localization, weaken the CI rate accordingly, or relabel that rung as an open-ended question on attainable generated-score drift.

Position ZhangBenMichaelImai2022SafePolicyRD in related_work as the closest RD cutoff policy-learning comparator and explain why selected local boundary value inference is orthogonal to its robust multiple-cutoff policy objective.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle: the kernel remains field-new, but comparator bookkeeping, nondegeneracy hypotheses, and the generated-score localization assumption need patching before Stage 0.
